author: gleaning
text:
Drop fs2 in favor of std file locking

fs2 was used in exactly one file (src/store_lock.rs) for exclusive flock acquire/release. std::fs::File::lock/unlock stabilized in Rust 1.89; the toolchain is 1.94 with no MSRV pin, so the std API is available unconditionally.

Swap lock_exclusive -> File::lock and FileExt::unlock -> File::unlock, then remove the dependency. Behavior is identical (both back onto flock(2) on Linux). Normal dep tree 41 -> 40 crates, no transitive regression. Part of the jq+git+ln footprint audit (epic bl-32f8).
